data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Aesthetic designs are perceived as more usable, which is evident in Spotify's clean interface.
data.technical_spec: Adopts a modern design language with a focus on clarity and engagement.

data.title: Fitts's Law
data.description: Design considers that the time to interact with elements depends on their size and distance.
data.technical_spec: Key controls and buttons are optimally placed for quick access and usability.

data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: Reduces decision-making time by simplifying options available at once.
data.technical_spec: Clear navigation with focused menus to streamline user journeys.

data.title: Jakob's Law
data.description: Users prefer interfaces that work similarly to those they are accustomed to.
data.technical_spec: Consistent design with familiar music-related features that users expect.

data.title: Miller's Law
data.description: Considers the capacity of short-term memory by limiting the number of elements in a group.
data.technical_spec: Organizes content in manageable chunks, enhancing focus and recall.
